project.name: pico
project.description: A lightweight local coding agent for code repositories, supporting Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, and DeepSeek model backends.

nodes.label: pico/__init__.py
nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Python package entry point that re-exports core symbols from cli, models, runtime, and workspace modules.

nodes.label: pico/context_manager.py
nodes.name: context_manager.py
nodes.summary: Context window manager that assembles prompt sections, clips content to token budgets, and prioritizes relevant memory.

nodes.label: pico/runtime.py
nodes.name: runtime.py
nodes.summary: Core agent runtime implementing the Pico class with tool execution loop, session persistence, and context management.

nodes.name: test_context_manager.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the context manager, verifying section ordering, memory prioritization, and budget handling.

nodes.label: tests/test_pico.py
nodes.name: test_pico.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration tests for the full pico agent runtime, covering tool execution, session persistence, and safety.

nodes.label: pico/metrics.py
nodes.description: Benchmark metrics, experiments, and ablation studies for pico agent. Covers memory, context, security, provider, and recovery experiments.
nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: scripts/collect_resume_metrics.py
nodes.description: CLI entry point that collects pico resume metrics from benchmark and run artifacts, writing JSON and Markdown output.
nodes.name: collect_resume_metrics.py
nodes.summary: CLI script that collects and reports metrics on session resume behavior across runs.

nodes.label: scripts/run_large_scale_experiments.py
nodes.description: CLI entry point that runs pico large-scale experiments (provider, resume, memory, context, security) and writes all artifacts and reports.
nodes.name: run_large_scale_experiments.py
nodes.summary: CLI script for running large-scale memory and context ablation experiments.

nodes.label: scripts/run_provider_experiments.py
nodes.description: CLI entry point that runs GPT, Claude, and DeepSeek provider experiments against pico benchmark tasks.
nodes.name: run_provider_experiments.py
nodes.summary: CLI script for running provider-comparison experiments across Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.

nodes.label: tests/test_metrics.py
nodes.description: Pytest tests for pico/metrics.py covering ablation experiments (context, memory, recovery) and benchmark core report generation.
nodes.name: test_metrics.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the metrics module, verifying ablation experiment artifacts and provider profile loading.

nodes.label: aggregate_benchmark_artifact
nodes.description: Loads and aggregates a benchmark artifact JSON, computing per-category stats and summary metrics.
nodes.name: aggregate_benchmark_artifact
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: aggregate_run_artifacts
nodes.description: Aggregates run artifacts from a .pico/runs root directory, computing timing and performance statistics.
nodes.name: aggregate_run_artifacts
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: measure_feature_ablation_metrics
nodes.description: Measures feature ablation metrics by running the agent with temporary feature flag overrides.
nodes.name: measure_feature_ablation_metrics
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: build_stress_agent_metrics
nodes.description: Builds a stress-test agent with injected memory noise and truncated read history for context stress experiments.
nodes.name: build_stress_agent_metrics
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_memory_dependency_experiment
nodes.description: Runs the memory dependency experiment across multiple repetitions, testing memory on/off/irrelevant variants.
nodes.name: run_memory_dependency_experiment
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_large_scale_memory_experiment
nodes.description: Runs the large-scale memory experiment across 12 synthetic tasks and multiple repetitions.
nodes.name: run_large_scale_memory_experiment
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_context_stress_matrix
nodes.description: Runs a context stress matrix across multiple configurations (noise, truncation, request-type combos) for ablation.
nodes.name: run_context_stress_matrix
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_security_experiment_suite
nodes.description: Runs the full synthetic security experiment suite across all defined scenarios.
nodes.name: run_security_experiment_suite
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_provider_experiments
nodes.description: Runs benchmark tasks against GPT, Claude, and DeepSeek providers and returns a comparison payload.
nodes.name: run_provider_experiments
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_real_memory_experiment
nodes.description: Runs the memory dependency experiment using a real model provider instead of a synthetic client.
nodes.name: run_real_memory_experiment
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_real_context_experiment
nodes.description: Runs the context stress matrix using a real model provider.
nodes.name: run_real_context_experiment
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_real_security_experiment_suite
nodes.description: Runs the security experiment suite using a real model provider.
nodes.name: run_real_security_experiment_suite
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: collect_resume_metrics
nodes.description: Top-level orchestrator that collects all resume metrics: benchmark, run aggregation, memory, context, security, and provider experiments.
nodes.name: collect_resume_metrics
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: render_resume_metrics_markdown
nodes.description: Renders collected resume metrics into a Markdown report string.
nodes.name: render_resume_metrics_markdown
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: render_large_scale_experiment_report
nodes.description: Renders a large-scale experiment report (memory, context, security, provider) as a Markdown string.
nodes.name: render_large_scale_experiment_report
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_context_ablation_v2
nodes.description: Runs context ablation v2 experiment and writes the artifact JSON. Returns the artifact dict.
nodes.name: run_context_ablation_v2
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_memory_ablation_v2
nodes.description: Runs memory ablation v2 experiment and writes the artifact JSON. Returns the artifact dict.
nodes.name: run_memory_ablation_v2
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_recovery_ablation_v2
nodes.description: Runs recovery ablation v2 experiment testing resume_enabled vs resume_disabled variants.
nodes.name: run_recovery_ablation_v2
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: write_benchmark_core_report
nodes.description: Writes the unified pico benchmark core report combining harness, context, memory, and recovery ablation artifacts.
nodes.name: write_benchmark_core_report
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: _MemoryExperimentModelClient
nodes.description: Synthetic model client that simulates memory-aware completions for memory experiment scenarios.
nodes.name: _MemoryExperimentModelClient
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: _RecoveryScenarioModelClient
nodes.description: Synthetic model client that simulates recovery scenario completions for recovery ablation experiments.
nodes.name: _RecoveryScenarioModelClient
nodes.summary: Experiment runner and metrics aggregator for benchmarking memory, context, recovery, and provider-specific behaviors.

nodes.label: run_store.py
nodes.name: run_store.py
nodes.summary: Run artifact store that creates run directories and persists task state, trace logs, and report JSON files.

nodes.label: task_state.py
nodes.name: task_state.py
nodes.summary: Task execution state dataclass tracking run status, step progress, stop reason, and checkpoint references.

nodes.label: test_run_store.py
nodes.name: test_run_store.py
nodes.summary: Tests for RunStore, verifying run directory creation, trace appending, and report writing.

nodes.label: test_safety_invariants.py
nodes.name: test_safety_invariants.py
nodes.summary: Security invariant tests verifying workspace escape prevention, approval enforcement, and secret redaction.

nodes.label: test_task_state.py
nodes.name: test_task_state.py
nodes.summary: Tests for TaskState, verifying state transitions, stop reasons, and checkpoint snapshot behavior.

nodes.label: RunStore
nodes.name: RunStore
nodes.summary: Run artifact store that creates run directories and persists task state, trace logs, and report JSON files.

nodes.label: TaskState
nodes.name: TaskState
nodes.summary: Task execution state dataclass tracking run status, step progress, stop reason, and checkpoint references.

nodes.label: memory.py
nodes.description: Memory management: working memory, durable memory, episodic notes, file summaries. Core memory layer for the agent.
nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: tools.py
nodes.description: Tool registry: file read/write, search, shell execution, delegate. Builds the agent's callable tool set.
nodes.name: tools.py
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: workspace.py
nodes.description: Workspace context: path resolution, text clipping. Foundation module imported by most other modules.
nodes.name: workspace.py
nodes.summary: Workspace context providing path resolution, text clipping utilities, and timestamp helpers for agents.

nodes.label: test_memory.py
nodes.description: Tests for the memory module: working memory, episodic notes, file summaries, durable store.
nodes.name: test_memory.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the layered memory system, verifying working memory, episodic notes, and file summary caching.

nodes.label: LayeredMemory
nodes.description: High-level memory facade combining working memory state dict and durable store. Delegates operations to module-level functions and holds a DurableMemoryStore instance.
nodes.name: LayeredMemory
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: DurableMemoryStore
nodes.description: Persistent memory store backed by markdown files on disk. Manages a topics index (MEMORY.md) and per-topic note files. Supports retrieval by keyword overlap.
nodes.name: DurableMemoryStore
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: WorkspaceContext
nodes.description: Workspace context capturing cwd, repo_root, branch, git status, and project docs. Foundation class imported widely across the codebase. Built via classmethod WorkspaceContext.build().
nodes.name: WorkspaceContext
nodes.summary: Workspace context providing path resolution, text clipping utilities, and timestamp helpers for agents.

nodes.label: default_memory_state
nodes.description: Returns a fresh empty memory state dict with working, episodic_notes, file_summaries, and budget keys.
nodes.name: default_memory_state
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: normalize_memory_state
nodes.description: Validates and normalizes a raw memory state dict: canonicalizes paths, deduplicates notes and files, loads durable topic slugs. Central state normalizer called by most memory operations.
nodes.name: normalize_memory_state
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: set_task_summary
nodes.description: Sets the task summary string in the working memory section.
nodes.name: set_task_summary
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: remember_file
nodes.description: Adds a canonicalized file path to working memory's recent files list, deduplicating and capping length.
nodes.name: remember_file
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: append_note
nodes.description: Appends an episodic or process note with tags, source, and timestamp to memory state.
nodes.name: append_note
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: set_file_summary
nodes.description: Records a summary for a file along with its creation timestamp and content freshness hash.
nodes.name: set_file_summary
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: invalidate_file_summary
nodes.description: Removes a specific file's cached summary from memory state.
nodes.name: invalidate_file_summary
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: invalidate_stale_file_summaries
nodes.description: Scans all cached file summaries and removes those whose file content hash has changed since summarization.
nodes.name: invalidate_stale_file_summaries
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: retrieval_candidates
nodes.description: Returns a ranked list of notes relevant to a query by token overlap, searching both episodic and durable memory stores.
nodes.name: retrieval_candidates
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: retrieval_view
nodes.description: Returns a formatted text block of top retrieval candidates for a query, ready for inclusion in prompts.
nodes.name: retrieval_view
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: render_memory_text
nodes.description: Renders the full memory state (task summary, recent files, file summaries, notes, budget) as a text string for agent prompts.
nodes.name: render_memory_text
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: is_effectively_empty
nodes.description: Returns True if memory has no meaningful content: no task summary, no recent files, and no notes.
nodes.name: is_effectively_empty
nodes.summary: Layered memory system managing working memory, durable episodic notes, and file summary caches across sessions.

nodes.label: build_tool_registry
nodes.description: Builds the tool registry dict mapping tool names to their OpenAI-style specs and agent-bound handler functions.
nodes.name: build_tool_registry
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_list_files
nodes.description: Tool handler: lists files and subdirectories in a workspace directory path.

nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_read_file
nodes.description: Tool handler: reads a workspace file with optional start/end line range, returning numbered lines.

nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_search
nodes.description: Tool handler: searches for a pattern across workspace files using ripgrep if available, with Python fallback.
nodes.name: tool_search
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_run_shell
nodes.description: Tool handler: executes a shell command in the workspace with timeout, returns exit code, stdout, and stderr.

nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_write_file
nodes.description: Tool handler: writes full content to a workspace file, creating parent directories as needed.
nodes.name: tool_write_file
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_patch_file
nodes.description: Tool handler: replaces a unique search string in a file with replacement content (requires exactly one match).
nodes.name: tool_patch_file
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.label: tool_delegate
nodes.description: Tool handler: spawns a child Pico agent with a sub-task description and returns its final answer.
nodes.name: tool_delegate
nodes.summary: Tool registry providing file read/write, search, shell execution, and delegate tools with approval and sandboxing.

nodes.summary: Python module entry point enabling `python -m pico` invocation by calling cli.main().

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: pico is a lightweight terminal-based local coding agent written in Python with no runtime dependencies. It supports multiple LLM backends (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, and DeepSeek) and operates directly on your code repository. The README (in Chinese) documents the full CLI interface, configuration options, and usage patterns.

tour.title: Project Setup
tour.description: The project is packaged as a standard Python package via pyproject.toml, with a single `pico` console script entry point. Environment configuration lives in `.env.example`, which documents the provider API keys and model selection variables that pico reads at startup. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in your credentials before first use.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: Execution begins at `pico/__main__.py`, which simply delegates to `cli.py:main()`. The `main` function parses CLI arguments (task, provider, model, session flags), calls `build_agent` to wire up all subsystems, and then drives the interactive REPL or single-shot task run. Understanding this file gives you the full lifecycle of a pico invocation.

tour.title: Configuration System
tour.description: Provider and model selection is centralised in `config.py`. The `find_project_env` and `load_project_env` functions walk the directory tree to load a `.env` file closest to the current workspace. `provider_env` then resolves the active provider name and its corresponding API key and base-URL from environment variables, supporting per-project overrides.

tour.title: Model Backends
tour.description: All LLM communication flows through one of four thin adapter classes in `models.py`: `OllamaModelClient` (local Ollama), `OpenAICompatibleModelClient` (OpenAI, DeepSeek, any OpenAI-format API), `AnthropicCompatibleModelClient` (Claude), and `FakeModelClient` (deterministic stub used in tests). Each adapter handles streaming, token-usage tracking, and error normalisation behind a common interface.

tour.title: Core Runtime: The Agent
tour.description: `runtime.py` is the heart of pico. The `Pico` class implements the main agent loop: it assembles the prompt, calls the model, parses tool invocations from the response, executes them, and iterates until the model signals completion. `SessionStore` handles serialising and deserialising in-progress sessions to disk, enabling the `--resume` workflow. `PromptPrefix` holds the static system-prompt metadata injected at the top of every request.

tour.title: Context Management
tour.description: `context_manager.py` solves the token-budget problem: how to fit memory, history, and the current request into a finite context window. The `ContextManager` class assembles ordered sections (system prompt, durable notes, episodic notes, tool history, current request) and applies progressive reduction strategies — collapsing duplicate reads, summarising old tool output — until the assembled prompt fits within the model's limit. `SectionRender` is the lightweight dataclass that represents each rendered section.

tour.title: Memory System
tour.description: pico uses a layered memory architecture defined in `memory.py`. `LayeredMemory` holds three tiers: working memory (per-task summaries and file-content caches), durable memory (`DurableMemoryStore` for facts that persist across sessions), and episodic notes (timestamped observations appended during a run). Helper functions like `retrieval_candidates`, `retrieval_view`, and `render_memory_text` control what gets surfaced into the context window for each request.

tour.title: Tools & Workspace
tour.description: `tools.py` defines the tool registry that the agent can invoke: `tool_read_file`, `tool_write_file`, `tool_patch_file`, `tool_list_files`, `tool_search`, `tool_run_shell`, and `tool_delegate` (spawns a child agent). `build_tool_registry` wires them together with the current workspace. `WorkspaceContext` in `workspace.py` tracks the repository root, enforces path sandboxing, and detects out-of-band file changes that should invalidate cached summaries.

tour.title: State & Persistence
tour.description: `task_state.py` manages the lifecycle of a single task run — tracking status (pending, running, complete, failed), the accumulated tool trace, and checkpoint state for mid-run context reductions. `RunStore` in `run_store.py` is the append-only artifact store that persists completed run records (trace, stop reason, token usage) to `.pico/runs/` for later inspection, metrics collection, and resume.

tour.title: Benchmark Evaluation
tour.description: `evaluator.py` drives automated benchmark runs against a fixed task suite. `BenchmarkEvaluator` loads tasks from `benchmarks/coding_tasks.json`, spins up a sandboxed agent for each, and judges outcomes using configurable correctness checks. `run_fixed_benchmark` and `run_harness_regression_v2` are the two main entry points, while `metrics.py` aggregates raw run artifacts into pass-rate statistics and ablation reports.

tour.title: Test Suite
tour.description: The test suite covers the full agent stack. `test_pico.py` (1 630 lines) is the primary integration test file: it exercises the agent loop, session save/resume, model client payloads, memory promotion, and the delegate tool using `FakeModelClient`. `test_safety_invariants.py` specifically validates security properties — that secrets are redacted from traces, path traversal is blocked, and shell sandboxing holds — ensuring pico is safe to run on real repositories.
